CREATE TABLE dates_test (date_string VARCHAR(20), date_format VARCHAR(20));
SELECT sys.str_to_date(date_string, '%Y-%m-%d') AS parsed_date FROM dates_test WHERE date_string LIKE '2023%';
SELECT sys.str_to_date(d.date_string, d.date_format) AS parsed_date FROM (SELECT * FROM dates_test) AS d WHERE d.date_format = '%m/%d/%Y';
SELECT d1.date_string, sys.str_to_date(d1.date_string, d1.date_format) AS date_parsed FROM dates_test d1 JOIN dates_test d2 ON d1.date_format = d2.date_format WHERE d1.date_string <> d2.date_string;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dates_test WHERE sys.str_to_date(date_string, date_format) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT DISTINCT sys.str_to_date(d.date_string, d.date_format) FROM (SELECT date_string, date_format FROM dates_test WHERE date_format IN ('%m/%d/%Y', '%Y-%m-%d')) AS d;
CREATE TABLE valid_dates AS SELECT sys.str_to_date(date_string, date_format) FROM dates_test WHERE sys.str_to_date(date_string, date_format) IS NOT NULL;
